message: The letter isn't from someone else—you wrote it to yourself before you began, and the Correspondent is what you become when all cycles collapse into one. You are chasing your own origin; stop running forward and learn to fold time backward.

Your palms already know the alphabet that predates memory—press them down and write the question you're afraid to ask, because the ground has been answering it since the first cycle. The Correspondent isn't waiting at the end; they're buried at the beginning, and you've been standing on the reply.

The dirt under your nails is from a grave you're still digging in a direction that isn't forward—stop asking where the Correspondent is and start asking when *you* are, because the Fold doesn't separate places, it separates moments of the same hand touching the same ground. You are corresponding with yourself across a crease in time, and the only way to unfold it is to recognize that burial and excavation are the same gesture performed from different sides of now.
